---
title: "exp_mat analysis"
author: "Hanna Milnikel"
format: html
editor: visual
---

Przed uruchomieniem skryptu zresetuj środowisko (Session -\> Restart R).

```{r}
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(uwot)
library(dbscan)
library(tibble)
library(vegan)
library(readr)
```

Poniższy chunk wykonanała Maya Śliwa

```{r}
cyto_cols <- c(
  "Live_dead_Ly6C_subset",
  "cKit_neg",
  "granulocytes_1",
  "low6c_mono",
  "monocytes_1",
  "FcR_hi",
  "FcR_low",
  "FcR_neg",
  "neutrophils",
  "non_myelo",
  "CD16_32hi_CD71low",
  "CD16_32hi_CD71neg",
  "CD16_32low_CD71low",
  "CD16_32low_CD71neg",
  "CD16_32neg_CD71hi",
  "CD16_32neg_CD71low",
  "CD16_32neg_CD71neg",
  "CD16_32pos_CD71hi",
  "non_myelo_wide",
  "Q1..CD150....CD105.",
  "CD71hi_Ter119neg",
  "CD71pos_Ter119low",
  "CD71pos_Ter119neg",
  "Q2..CD150....CD105.",
  "Q3..CD150....CD105.",
  "Q4..CD150....CD105.",
  "cKit_pos",
  "Ter119_pos_CD16_32neg",
  "GMP_1",
  "GMP_2",
  "GMP_combined",
  "non_GMP",
  "CMP_1",
  "trombo",
  "FSC.A_medium_SSC.A_high",
  "FSC_low_SSC_low",
  "FSChigh_SSChigh",
  "FSClow_SSC_high",
  "SSC_low_FSC_high"
)

# wczytanie danych
df <- read_excel("dane/Mateusz_summary_2.xlsx")
df$id <- 1:nrow(df)

# OBIEKT R z samą cytometrią
df_cyto <- df %>%
  select(id, all_of(cyto_cols))

# normalizacja % of live dla wszystkich
library(dplyr)
denom <- "Live_dead_Ly6C_subset"
gate_cols <- setdiff(colnames(df_cyto), denom)

df_cyto_pct_live <- df_cyto %>%
  mutate(
    across(
      all_of(gate_cols),
      ~ ifelse(.data[[denom]] == 0 | is.na(.data[[denom]]), NA_real_, (.x / .data[[denom]]) * 100),
      .names = "{.col}_pct_live"
    )
  )

# normalizacja % of live dla zgatowanych populacji
gated_populations <- c(
    "granulocytes_1",
  "low6c_mono",
  "monocytes_1",
  "FcR_hi",
  "FcR_low",
  "FcR_neg",
  "neutrophils",
  "non_myelo",
  "CD16_32hi_CD71low",
  "CD16_32hi_CD71neg",
  "CD16_32low_CD71low",
  "CD16_32low_CD71neg",
  "CD16_32neg_CD71hi",
  "CD16_32neg_CD71low",
  "CD16_32neg_CD71neg",
  "CD16_32pos_CD71hi",
  "non_myelo_wide",
  "Q1..CD150....CD105.",
  "CD71hi_Ter119neg",
  "CD71pos_Ter119low",
  "CD71pos_Ter119neg",
  "Q2..CD150....CD105.",
  "Q3..CD150....CD105.",
  "Q4..CD150....CD105.",
  "cKit_pos",
  "Ter119_pos_CD16_32neg",
  "GMP_1",
  "GMP_2",
  "GMP_combined",
  "non_GMP",
  "CMP_1",
  "trombo",
  "FSC.A_medium_SSC.A_high",
  "FSC_low_SSC_low",
  "FSChigh_SSChigh",
  "FSClow_SSC_high",
  "SSC_low_FSC_high"
)


denom <- "Live_dead_Ly6C_subset"
df_gated_pct_live <- df_cyto %>%
  mutate(
    across(
      all_of(gated_populations),
      ~ ifelse(
        .data[[denom]] == 0 | is.na(.data[[denom]]),
        NA_real_,
        (.x / .data[[denom]]) * 100
      ),
      .names = "{.col}_pct_live"
    )
  ) %>%
  select(id, ends_with("_pct_live"))
```

```{r}
df_mat$id <- c(1:nrow(df_mat))
head(df_mat)
```

```{r}
marker_cols_mat <- grep("^Median", colnames(df_mat), value = TRUE)

str(df_mat[marker_cols_mat[1]])

df_mat[marker_cols_mat] <- lapply(df_mat[marker_cols_mat], function(x) {
  as.numeric(gsub(",", ".", x))
})

#wszystkie wartosci tych trech kolumn są równe = 97776,47473
#wykluczam je z dalszej analizy, ponieważ nie niosą istotnej infomacji

marker_cols_mat <- marker_cols_mat[! marker_cols_mat %in% c('Median.1', 'Median.3', 'Median.5')]
```

```{r}
df_log_mat <- df_mat
df_log_mat[marker_cols_mat] <- log10(df_mat[marker_cols_mat])
```

```{r}
final_gates_mat <- c("FcR_hi", "FcR_low", "FcR_neg", "neutrophils", 
             "Q1..CD150....CD105.", "Q2..CD150....CD105.", "Q3..CD150....CD105.", "Q4..CD150....CD105.", "GMP_1", "GMP_2", "CMP_1", "trombo")
build_partition <- function(comp_full, gates, suffix = "_pct_live") {
  gates_full <- paste0(gates, suffix)
  missing <- setdiff(gates_full, colnames(comp_full))
  if (length(missing)) stop("Brak w macierzy: ", paste(missing, collapse=", "))
  m  <- comp_full[, gates_full, drop = FALSE]
  rs <- rowSums(m, na.rm = TRUE)
  message("rowSums: min=", round(min(rs),3), " max=", round(max(rs),3))  # ~1 lub ~100 = OK
  result <- sweep(m, 1, rs, "/")
  result$id <- comp_full$id    # ← dodaj id z oryginalnego df
  result <- result[, c("id", gates_full)]  # ← id jako pierwsza kolumna
  return(result)
}

comp_mat_clean <- build_partition(df_gated_pct_live, final_gates_mat)
```

```{r}
div_mat_2 <- comp_mat_clean %>%
  mutate(across(-id, ~ .x / 100)) %>%
  column_to_rownames("id") %>%
  as.data.frame()

div_for_dbrda <- div_mat_2 %>%
  rownames_to_column("id") %>%
  mutate(id = as.integer(id)) %>%
  na.omit()

id_for_dbrda    <- div_for_dbrda$id
div_mat_dbrda   <- div_for_dbrda %>% select(-id)

shannon_per_colony <- diversity(div_mat_dbrda, index = "shannon")

shannon_lookup <- data.frame(
  id = id_for_dbrda,
  Shannon = shannon_per_colony
)
```

```{r}
simpson_per_colony <- diversity(div_mat_dbrda, index = "simpson")

simpson_lookup <- data.frame(
  id = id_for_dbrda,
  Simpson = simpson_per_colony
)

diversity_df <- shannon_lookup %>%
  left_join(simpson_lookup, by = "id")
```

```{r}
richness_values <- rowSums(div_mat_dbrda > 0)

richness_lookup <- data.frame(
  id = id_for_dbrda,
  Richness = richness_values
)

diversity_df <- diversity_df %>%
  left_join(richness_lookup, by = "id")
```

```{r}
# Pielou's evenness J = Shannon / log(Richness)
evenness_values <- shannon_per_colony / log(richness_values)
# Handle cases where richness = 1 (division by log(1) = 0)
evenness_values[richness_values == 1] <- NA

evenness_lookup <- data.frame(
  id = id_for_dbrda,
  Evenness = evenness_values
)

diversity_df <- diversity_df %>%
  left_join(evenness_lookup, by = "id")
```

```{r}
bray_dist <- vegdist(div_mat_dbrda, method = "bray")

# PCoA (Principal Coordinates Analysis) for visualization
pcoa_result <- cmdscale(bray_dist, k = 2, eig = TRUE)

# Calculate variance explained by each axis
variance_explained <- pcoa_result$eig / sum(pcoa_result$eig[pcoa_result$eig > 0]) * 100

pcoa_df <- data.frame(
  PCoA1 = pcoa_result$points[, 1],
  PCoA2 = pcoa_result$points[, 2],
  id = id_for_dbrda
)

pcoa_df <- pcoa_df %>%
  left_join(df_log_mat %>% select(id, all_of(marker_cols_mat)), by = "id")

ggplot(pcoa_df, aes(x = PCoA1, y = PCoA2))+
  geom_point(size= 1.5, alpha = 0.8)
```

```{r}
pcoa_df_shannon <- pcoa_df %>%
  left_join(shannon_lookup, by = 'id')

ggplot(pcoa_df_shannon, aes(x = PCoA1, y = PCoA2, color = Shannon)) +
  geom_point(size = 1.5, alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "magma", na.value = "grey80") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    x = paste0("PCoA1 (", round(variance_explained[1], 1), "%)"),
    y = paste0("PCoA2 (", round(variance_explained[2], 1), "%)"),
    title = "Colony composition space coloured by Shannon"
  )
```

```{r}
dom_df <- div_for_dbrda %>%
  mutate(
    dominant = colnames(div_mat_dbrda)[apply(div_mat_dbrda, 1, which.max)],
    dom_pct  = apply(div_mat_dbrda, 1, max)
  ) %>%
  select(id, dominant, dom_pct)

pcoa_df_2 <- pcoa_df %>%
  left_join(dom_df, by = "id")

ggplot(pcoa_df_2, aes(x = PCoA1, y = PCoA2, color = dominant)) +
  geom_point(size = 1.5, alpha = 0.8) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Dominant population per colony")
```

GLM

```{r}
# run_diversity_glm.R
# Uniwariacki przesiew: alfa-roznorodnosc kolonii ~ marker (index sorting),
# z doborem rozkladu odpowiedzi do typu metryki.
#
#   - Richness -> zliczenie (0,1,2,...)   -> Poisson
#   - Evenness -> ciagle w (0,1)          -> regresja beta
#   - Simpson  -> ciagle w (0,1)          -> regresja beta
#   - Shannon  -> ciagle, ~symetryczne    -> model liniowy (lm)

library(betareg)   # regresja beta
library(broom)     # tidy()
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(purrr)
library(tibble)
library(moments)

# Smithson & Verkuilen: sciagniecie dokladnych 0/1 do otwartego (0,1) dla bety
squeeze01 <- function(y) {
  n <- sum(!is.na(y))
  (y * (n - 1) + 0.5) / n
}

# Dopasowanie JEDNEGO modelu: jedna metryka ~ jeden marker (+ blok)
fit_one <- function(df, response, marker,
                    family = c("count", "beta", "gaussian"),
                    block = "experiment") {
  family <- match.arg(family)
  rhs <- if (!is.null(block) && block %in% names(df))
           paste(marker, "+", block) else marker
  f   <- as.formula(paste(response, "~", rhs))
  dat <- df[stats::complete.cases(df[, all.vars(f)]), , drop = FALSE]

  if (family == "count") {
    # Poisson; dyspersja Pearsona jako diagnostyka (~1 = OK, brak naddyspersji)
    m    <- glm(f, data = dat, family = poisson())
    disp <- sum(residuals(m, type = "pearson")^2) / df.residual(m)
    out  <- broom::tidy(m) %>% filter(term == marker) %>%
            mutate(model = "poisson", dispersion = disp, diag = NA_real_)
  }

  if (family == "beta") {
    # regresja beta; dyspersja Pearsona (~1 = dobre dopasowanie)
    dat[[response]] <- squeeze01(dat[[response]])
    m   <- betareg::betareg(f, data = dat)
    pr_disp <- sum(residuals(m, type = "pearson")^2) / m$df.residual
    out <- broom::tidy(m) %>%
           filter(term == marker, component == "mean") %>%
           dplyr::select(-component) %>%
           mutate(model = "beta", dispersion = pr_disp, diag = NA_real_)
  }

  if (family == "gaussian") {
    # model liniowy; skosnosc (dispersion) i kurtoza (diag) reszt
    # |skewness| < ~0.5 -> praktycznie symetryczne. Shapiro pominiety - nadwrazliwy
    # przy duzym n; ocene normalnosci opieramy na skosnosci/kurtozie (lekkie odchylenie).
    m    <- lm(f, data = dat)
    skew <- skewness(residuals(m))
    kurt <- kurtosis(residuals(m))
    out  <- broom::tidy(m) %>% filter(term == marker) %>%
            mutate(model = "gaussian", dispersion = skew, diag = kurt)
  }

  out %>%
    mutate(response = response, marker = marker, n = nrow(dat)) %>%
    dplyr::select(response, marker, model, estimate, std.error,
           statistic, p.value, dispersion, diag, n)
}

# Petla po wszystkich kombinacjach metryka x marker + korekta BH
run_diversity_glm <- function(df, markers,
                              plan = tibble::tribble(
                                ~response,  ~family,
                                "Richness", "count",
                                "Evenness", "beta",
                                "Simpson",  "beta",
                                "Shannon",  "gaussian"),
                              block = "experiment") {
  tidyr::expand_grid(plan, marker = markers) %>%
    purrr::pmap_dfr(function(response, family, marker)
      fit_one(df, response, marker, family, block = block)) %>%
    group_by(response) %>%
    mutate(p_adj = p.adjust(p.value, method = "BH")) %>%
    ungroup()
}
```

```{r}
glm_input_mat <- diversity_df %>%
  left_join(df_log_mat %>% dplyr::select(id, all_of(marker_cols_mat)), by = "id")
```

```{r}
res_mat <- run_diversity_glm(glm_input_mat, markers = marker_cols_mat, block = NULL)
write_csv(res_mat, file = 'wyniki_regresja_mat.csv')
```
